title: Intensely Umami Jamaican Ground Beef Hash
description: A Mediterranean-friendly, meal-prep Jamaican dish featuring ground pistachios and a sweet glaze, cooked to perfection in an iron teppan.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan to medium-high heat, approximately 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine ground beef, ground pistachios, onion, scotch bonnet pepper, thyme, allspice, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Add the soy sauce and brown sugar to the bowl and mix until the ingredients are evenly distributed.
Brush the preheated iron teppan with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Add the beef mixture to the iron teppan, breaking it up with a spatula as it cooks.
Cook the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is fully browned and cooked through.
Use the spatula to shape the mixture into a flat, even layer, allowing it to develop a crust on the bottom.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the crust is golden brown and the mixture is heated through.
Remove the iron teppan from the heat and let the hash cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the intensely umami Jamaican ground beef hash hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
